title: compare_flood_narratives data payload
description:
Structured payload for the `compare_flood_narratives` tool — returns an episode × witness alignment matrix for the four major Ancient Near Eastern flood narratives.

Witnesses: Sumerian Ziusudra story (Nippur, OB), Akkadian Atra-ḫasīs (Lambert & Millard 1969), Gilgamesh Tablet XI (George 2003), Hebrew Genesis 6-9 (BHS / MT). Episodes: a controlled vocabulary of canonical scenes (`overpopulation`, `divine_decision`, `forewarning`, `ark_construction`, `the_flood`, `landfall`, `sacrifice`, `aftermath`, `new_order`).

Alignment is by *episode*, not by line. Each cell carries the passage's standard citation, a translation excerpt, the divergence_notes between this witness and the canonical version, and a scholarly_anchor pointing to the modern critical edition. Use this for comparative-religion work, source-criticism arguments, and Genesis-6-9 background.

Provenance is `source: local`, `endpoint: local:floodNarrativeIndex`. The underlying alignments are derived from the Research/Atrahasis.md brief plus Lambert & Millard 1969, George 2003, Westermann 1984 — each cell carries its specific reference under `scholarly_anchor`.

episodes.description: Episodes covered in this response. Subset of the controlled vocabulary, in canonical narrative order.

witnesses.description: Witnesses (textual sources) covered in this response. Subset of the canonical four.

alignment.description: Array of episode-rows. Each row contains aligned cells indexed by witness id. A missing cell means the episode is absent or unrecoverably damaged in that witness — explicit `null` instead of omission makes the negative information visible.

episode.description: Episode id from the controlled vocabulary.

summary.description: Brief synthetic description of what this episode is (one sentence). Helps consumers without comparative-religion background read the matrix.

cells.description: Map of witness-id → cell. Keys are from WitnessId enum. Values may be `null` to mark explicit absence.

divergence_summary.description: One-paragraph synthesis of how the witnesses differ on this episode. Optional — present where the divergence is theologically or philologically load-bearing (e.g., flood duration, post-flood resolution).

WitnessId.description: Canonical witness id. `sumerian_ziusudra` = Nippur OB flood story; `atrahasis` = Lambert & Millard 1969 standard text; `gilgamesh_xi` = George 2003 SB Tablet XI; `genesis_6_9` = Masoretic Text (BHS).

Cell.description: Single witness's testimony for one episode.

citation.description: Standard citation in the witness's own reference system. E.g. `Atra-ḫasīs III.i.1-15`, `Gilgamesh XI.20-31`, `Genesis 6:13-16`, `Sumerian Flood Story 145-155`.

excerpt.description: Translation excerpt of the passage. Length capped at ~6 lines/~600 chars. Use the standard published translation cited under `scholarly_anchor`.

translator.description: Translator/edition the excerpt is taken from. E.g. `Lambert & Millard 1969`, `George 2003`, `Foster 2005`, `NRSV`.

scholarly_anchor.description: Specific modern critical edition or commentary anchoring this passage. Format: `Author Year, page-or-line range`. E.g. `Lambert & Millard 1969, p.92-94`.

divergence_notes.description: Bullet-point notes on how THIS witness's account of this episode differs from sibling witnesses. Optional. E.g. `Flood lasts 7 days here; Genesis has 40 days`.

philological_uncertainty.description: Confidence in the textual basis. `secure` = well-preserved manuscript; `partial` = readable with gaps; `fragmentary` = small surviving fragment; `reconstructed` = restored from parallels. Sumerian Ziusudra cells are mostly `partial` or `fragmentary`.
